div.h1: Stack Frame Layout & Frame Pointer Chaining
div.p: Frame pointer chain enables stack unwinding by creating a linked list through the stack

g.text: Frame Pointer Chain (Linked List in Memory)
RBP | Frame Base | Points to saved_rbp
RSP | Stack Ptr | Points to top of stack
RIP | Instr Ptr | Current execution addr
-fomit-frame-pointer | +2-5% perf | +1 free register | No stack traces
-fno-omit-frame-pointer | -2-5% perf | RBP reserved | Full stack traces
-fno-omit-leaf-frame-pointer | -1-2% perf | Leaf frames ok | Partial traces
rbp = *rbp; // follow chain: RBP → saved RBP
mov rbp, rsp ; establish our frame base

text.tspan: // When frame pointers unavailable, use DWARF CFI
// .eh_frame section describes how to unwind at each IP
// Cons: 5-20x slower, complex parsing, may not be signal-safe
// When frame pointers unavailable, use DWARF CFI
// .eh_frame section describes how to unwind at each IP
// Cons: 5-20x slower, complex parsing, may not be signal-safe
; RBP is now a general-purpose register!
; Used for: loop counter, cached value, etc.
; PROBLEM: No saved_rbp chain to follow!
; RBP is now a general-purpose register!
; Used for: loop counter, cached value, etc.
; PROBLEM: No saved_rbp chain to follow!
; RBP always points to valid saved_rbp
